mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate directly reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of declining rate months.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month cumulative increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals severe labor market deterioration that immediately suppresses consumer spending expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance with p-value greater than 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in consumer price inflation and declines in real personal savings deposits indicate stagflationary pressure that compresses corporate profit margins and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce negative returns in more than 55% of occurrences or the interaction term loses predictive power in multivariate regression models.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index readings above the 100 baseline indicate robust manufacturing capacity utilization that supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Short-term returns turn negative in more than 65% of instances when INDPRO exceeds 100 or the signal decays to zero predictive accuracy within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts reflect expanding residential construction activity and positive household wealth effects that consistently drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of rising housing start months or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 300 trigger immediate market expectations of aggressive monetary tightening that raises equity discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 300 or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor demand that expands corporate earnings capacity and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 55% of occurrences or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate models.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth rates below 0.5 percent signal economic contraction that immediately triggers risk-off positioning and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Short-term returns turn positive in more than 60% of low-GDP quarters or the signal loses predictive accuracy during non-recessionary periods.
